conclusion: Red c9 grouped-compact rowchunk2 stress followed by explicit c2 serial_lm_head sampling did not reproduce the active-c2 [103,137] flake: five c9 grouped runs were the known red [137,137,137,118,137,31,81,137,137], but all five following c2 serial-sampler runs were [137,137]. Compared with iter222 default batched-sampler c2 after c9 grouped stress reproducing [103,137] in 1/3 cycles, this narrows the trigger toward post-c9 stress interacting with the default batched LM-head sampler path rather than upstream c2 hidden/decode state alone. Because the phenomenon is nondeterministic, this is not proof of causality. No throughput claim is made.
remaining_blockers: repeat the c9 grouped stress with default batched-sampler c2 and serial-sampler c2 interleaved if stronger sampler interaction evidence is needed
isolate whether the batched LM-head sampler artifact/path has row-state or resource sensitivity after c9 grouped red work
active c2 retained readiness remains blocked until the intermittent [103,137] flake is explained or retired with stronger evidence
full-native/no-rowchunk grouped full-attention remains the retained c4/c8 blocker

workload.cycle_command_shape: for each cycle: run c9 --batch-size 9 --batch-decode-full-attn-row-chunk-size 2 --batch-decode-moe-path grouped_compact, then immediately run c2 --batch-size 2 --batch-sample-mode serial_lm_head in a new Python process
